title: Quarter-note foundations
description: Find a steady pulse and learn to read the spaces as confidently as the notes.

guide.text: Music is read left to right, like words. Each bar holds four steady beats: count them out loud, 1 2 3 4. A quarter note (crotchet) means play on that beat. A quarter rest (crotchet rest) means stay silent for that beat, but keep counting. The beat does not stop just because you do.

title: Around the kit
description: Read the same quarter-note language as it moves between hi-hat, snare and kick.

guide.text: Each drum lives on its own line or space. The hi-hat sits at the top with an x-shaped head, the snare sits in the middle, and the kick lives at the bottom. Same rhythms as level 1: your eyes now check which drum as well as when.

title: Eighth notes with quarters
description: Split each beat neatly in two, then combine eighth-note pairs with the quarter notes you already know.

guide.text: 8th notes (quavers) split each beat in half: count 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 &. Two 8ths are worth one quarter note and are joined by a beam. The & lands exactly halfway between beats, so keep your counting even, not rushed.

title: Quarters and eighth-note pairs
description: Switch confidently between one note per beat and two equal notes per beat.

guide.text: Read one beat at a time: each beat is either a quarter note or a pair of 8ths. Say the counts out loud as you play.

title: Layered eighths
description: Keep the eighth-note pulse steady while kick and snare rhythms sit underneath it.

guide.text: When voices layer up, keep counting out loud and let each limb join the count one at a time.

title: Eighth-note rests
description: Keep the pulse steady while notes and spaces move between the two halves of each beat.

guide.text: An 8th rest (quaver rest) silences half a beat. When the rest is on the beat, you play only the &. Count every syllable out loud and whisper the silent ones.

key.label: 8th rest on the beat, play the &

title: Quarter rests with eighth notes
description: Keep counting through full-beat silences while eighth-note phrases continue around them.

guide.text: Rests are counted, not skipped. Keep the pulse through every silence.

title: Eighth-note rests around the kit
description: Read the gaps across the kit, then let the hands and feet fill each other's spaces.

guide.text: Same 8th-note rests, now moving around the kit. Read the voice first, then the rhythm.

title: Quarter and eighth rests around the kit
description: Read full-beat and half-beat silences as the hands and feet move around one another.

guide.text: Quarter and 8th rests mixed around the kit. Slow down and count: silence is part of the groove.

key.label: Quarter and eighth rests around the kit

title: Sixteenth-note foundations
description: Divide each beat into four and keep the strokes even through familiar full-beat gaps.

guide.text: 16th notes (semiquavers) split each beat into four: count 1 e & a. Four 16ths fit in one quarter note and are joined by a double beam. Slow is your friend: precision first, speed later.

title: Eighths and sixteenths
description: Switch cleanly between two and four notes per beat without adding rests or quarter notes yet.

guide.text: 8ths and 16ths side by side. The beam tells you the speed: single beam for 8ths, double beam for 16ths.

title: Quarters, eighths and sixteenths
description: Combine one, two and four notes per beat before bringing silence back into the phrase.

guide.text: Quarters, 8ths and 16ths together. Read each beat as one shape before you play it.

title: Mixed values with quarter rests
description: Combine all three sounding note values with full-beat quarter rests.

guide.text: Full-beat silences inside busy rhythms. Keep counting 1 e & a through every rest.

title: Mixed values with eighth rests
description: Add half-beat rests to the mixed-value vocabulary while keeping every silence inside its beat.

guide.text: 8th rests inside mixed rhythms silence half a beat. Find which syllables are silent before you play.

title: Mixed values with sixteenth rests
description: Complete the binary-rest vocabulary with single sixteenth-note gaps in mixed phrases.

guide.text: A 16th rest (semiquaver rest) silences a quarter of a beat. Keep counting 1 e & a and play only the syllables with notes.

title: Mixed binary rhythms around the kit
description: Apply quarter, eighth and sixteenth-note rhythms and rests across the hands and feet.

guide.text: Everything binary, everywhere on the kit. Scan each bar for its trickiest beat before you start.

key.label: Mixed binary rhythms around the kit

title: Triplets and mixed rhythms
description: Feel three equal parts in a beat, then switch confidently between triplet and straight subdivisions.

guide.text: A triplet (quaver triplets) fits three even notes into one beat where two 8ths would normally go, marked with a 3. Count 1-trip-let. The feel is rounder: three equal parts, not a rhythm you already know squeezed in.

key.label: Triplet: three even notes, one beat
